name: Sewage Treatment Plant SCADA
description: Compromise the SCADA system of a municipal wastewater treatment plant to manipulate chemical dosing, bypass disinfection, or release untreated sewage — causing environmental contamination and public health risks.
root_objective: Manipulate sewage treatment SCADA to cause environmental contamination

nodes.title: Cause Environmental Contamination via Sewage Plant SCADA
nodes.description: Gain control of the wastewater treatment SCADA system to disable chlorine/UV disinfection, manipulate chemical dosing, or open diversion valves to release raw sewage into waterways.

nodes.title: Remote Access via Cellular/VPN Gateway
nodes.description: Compromise the remote access mechanisms (cellular modem, VPN appliance) that connect the treatment plant to the utility's central SCADA system.

nodes.title: Direct Attack on Plant HMI and PLCs
nodes.description: Gain physical or network access to the on-site SCADA HMI and PLCs controlling pumps, valves, chemical feed systems, and aeration blowers.

nodes.title: Exploit Internet-Exposed HMI/VNC Interface
nodes.description: Many small treatment plants expose HMI workstations via VNC or RDP directly on the internet. Scan for and access these using default credentials or brute-force.

nodes.title: Compromise Cellular Modem with Default Credentials
nodes.description: Access the plant's Sierra Wireless/Digi/Cradlepoint cellular modem using factory defaults to gain access to the OT LAN behind it.

nodes.title: Disable Chlorine Dosing System
nodes.description: Issue commands to the chlorine/sodium hypochlorite dosing PLC to shut off chemical feed pumps, allowing untreated effluent to be discharged.

nodes.title: Physical Access to Unmanned Off-Hours Plant
nodes.description: Enter the treatment plant during off-hours when no operators are present. Many smaller plants are unmanned overnight with minimal access controls.

nodes.title: Manipulate Chemical Feed Rates to Toxic Levels
nodes.description: Increase sodium hypochlorite or ferric chloride dosing rates to dangerous levels, creating toxic chemical conditions in the treatment process or downstream waterway.

nodes.title: Open Bypass Valve for Raw Sewage Discharge
nodes.description: Command the influent bypass valve to open, diverting raw untreated sewage directly to the plant outfall and into the receiving waterway.

nodes.title: HMI Directly Exposed to Internet
nodes.description: Small municipal water/wastewater utilities frequently expose SCADA HMI workstations to the internet via VNC, RDP, or TeamViewer for remote operator access, with weak or no authentication.

nodes.title: Flat OT Network Without Segmentation
nodes.description: The plant's OT network has no segmentation — HMI, PLCs, chemical controllers, and the remote access gateway all share a single network segment.

nodes.title: Cellular Modem Provides Internet-to-OT Bridge
nodes.description: The cellular modem acts as the sole gateway between the internet and the flat OT network, making it the critical pivot point for remote attackers.
